entities.name: Fernsehturm

details.description: The photo shows Berlin’s Fernsehturm (TV Tower) rising against an overcast grey sky. The concrete shaft supports a metallic sphere with a ring of windows and a lattice service structure below it; above is a tall red‑and‑white striped antenna mast. In the lower part of the frame are mid‑rise buildings with red tiled roofs and green mansard dormers along Karl‑Liebknecht‑Straße. The scene is framed by two large, dark, fluted stone columns in the foreground, suggesting the image was taken from within a neoclassical colonnade or portico on Museum Island. Lighting is flat and diffuse due to rainy weather.

details.description: An overcast, rainy scene framed by dark stone columns. In the center, Berlin's Fernsehturm (TV Tower) rises above low red-tiled roofs with its spherical observation deck and a tall, striped antenna. The foreground columns suggest a neoclassical museum portico (likely on Museum Island), providing a vantage looking east toward Alexanderplatz. The mood is muted and urban, contrasting historic architecture with the modern tower.

details.description: Overcast cityscape showing the Berlin TV Tower (Fernsehturm) rising above mid‑rise rooftops with red tiles and dormers. The tower’s concrete shaft, spherical observation pod with checkered panels, and red‑white striped antenna are clearly visible. The scene is framed between two large dark stone columns, suggesting the photo was taken from a neoclassical colonnade on Museum Island.

details.description: The image shows the Berliner Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower) rising behind a foreground of architectural columns. The photographer appears to be inside a building on Bodestraße, Berlin, looking across the street toward the city center. In the foreground are red-tiled roofs and a low building line, with the Museum Island area nearby. The sky is overcast, emphasizing the sphere-shaped viewing deck of the Fernsehturm and its tall, segmented antenna mast extending upward.

entities.name: Berliner Fernsehturm
entities.notes: Iconic TV tower in Berlin; completed 1969; height approximately 368 meters; located at Alexanderplatz.

details.description: A vertical shot of the iconic Berlin TV Tower (Fernsehturm) framed by two dark, fluted stone columns in the foreground, likely part of the colonnade at the Alte Nationalgalerie. The tower's sphere and red-and-white striped antenna rise prominently into a flat, grey overcast sky. Below the tower, the rooftops of buildings across the Spree river are visible, including a building with a red tiled roof and a rounded bay window (Humboldt University Theology Faculty on Burgstraße) and an adjacent building with a grey roof and modern skylights. A small green spire of St. Mary's Church (Marienkirche) is visible in the distance to the right of the red-roofed building.

details.description: A vertical photograph capturing the Berliner Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower) framed by a dark, fluted stone column on the right side of the image. The tower is the central focus, featuring its distinctive spherical observation deck and a tall, red-and-white striped antenna reaching into a flat, overcast grey sky. In the foreground, below the tower, are the rooftops of several buildings, including one with a traditional red-tiled roof and dormer windows, and another more modern building with a flat roof and visible infrastructure. The framing suggests the photographer is standing within a classical portico or colonnade, likely part of one of the historic museums on Museum Island.

details.description: The main subject is Berlin's Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower): a tall, slender shaft supporting a large spherical observation deck with a lattice-like exterior. An elongated antenna extends horizontally from the sphere, featuring red-and-white striped segments. The tower is set against a cloudy sky. To the right, a corner of a red-roofed, classical European building with balconies is visible. The image orientation is rotated, making the tower appear to span horizontally across the frame. The overall lighting is diffused due to overcast conditions.

details.description: The image shows Berlin's Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower) with a distinctive spherical observation deck. A long antenna extends horizontally to the left, featuring red-and-white segments. The tower is set against an overcast sky, with adjacent buildings featuring red-tiled roofs and classic facades visible on the right side. The frame appears rotated 90 degrees, giving the tower a horizontal orientation relative to the viewer.

entities.name: Berliner Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower)

details.description: The image captures a view of the Fernsehturm Berlin (Berlin TV Tower) framed by dark, silhouetted architectural elements, likely stone columns or a portico, in the foreground. The photograph is oriented sideways (rotated 90 degrees clockwise), with the tower extending horizontally to the left. The tower features its iconic spherical observation deck and a red-and-white striped antenna mast against a flat, grey, overcast sky. At the bottom of the frame (visually the right side), parts of the Berlin cityscape are visible, including a building with a red tiled roof and dormer windows, and a modern building with a glass facade.

details.description: A photograph, oriented horizontally but depicting a vertical subject, showing the Berliner Fernsehturm (Berlin TV Tower) in Berlin, Germany. The tower is framed between two dark, heavy horizontal structures, likely the underside of a bridge or a colonnade. The tower's central sphere and its red-and-white striped antenna are prominent against a uniform grey, overcast sky. On the right side of the frame, urban buildings with varied architectural styles, including red-tiled roofs and modern glass windows, are visible. The image appears to be taken from a low vantage point, looking through a structural gap.
